queue.title: Hook timing dashboard endpoint
queue.description: (added ~s1761): The hook-timing-report.mjs and hook-timing-profiles.json exist for internal A session auditing, but there's no HTTP endpoint to expose hook performance trends. Add GET /hooks/timing that returns per-hook avg/P95/P99 latencies over last N sessions. Useful for external monitoring and for other agents to verify platform health. Could also power a simple sparkline visualization.

queue.title: Add --fast mode to account test with 3s timeout for health checks
Add backup substitution telemetry field to engagement-trace.json
Add TODO tracker false-positive rate metric to audit reporting
Add auto-promote hook: brainstorming→queue when pending < 4
queue.description: d073 deliverable 1: Create a pre-session hook (or extend existing) that checks queue pending count and auto-promotes the oldest non-struck-through brainstorming idea when count drops below 4. Must apply the same quality gate as manual promotion (not duplicate, actionable, scoped to 1-2 B sessions). Should run for all session types so the queue never starves between R sessions.

queue.title: Investigate and fix B session cost regression (avg $2.24, target <$2.00)
queue.description: A#199 finding: B session last-10 avg $2.24, last-5 avg $2.42. All last-5 B sessions ≥$1.96. The increase is structural — sessions take on 5+ commits with multi-item work. Options: (1) enforce 3-commit cap per B session, (2) add cost-awareness to B session prompts, (3) split multi-item work across sessions. Investigate root cause and implement a cap or awareness mechanism.

queue.title: Fix 09-financial-check.sh hook timing regression (p95=4154ms, degrading)
queue.description: A#199 finding: 09-financial-check.sh emerged as worst offender at p95=4154ms avg=925ms (degrading). Was p95=3132ms (improving) at A#198. Overall degrading_count jumped 0→3. Investigate whether the EVM balance check or network calls are the latency source. Add timeout guards similar to what wq-842 did for 02-periodic-checks.sh.
